mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed zero during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings combined with falling interest rates indicates capital seeking yield in equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns when RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS falls, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy economic expansion without overheating, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.4% monthly growth, or quarterly forward returns are not significantly positive during low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts directly increase construction sector revenues and consumer wealth effects, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample, or HOUST increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day returns in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% imposes financing constraints on corporate borrowing and compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes when inflation is above 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total employment and real savings indicates robust household balance sheets that fuel consumer demand, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k, or forward returns are negative when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation, declining industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL is below 230.0, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45% during high-inflation periods.
